name: ExtensionAttachment_Frames
title: R5: Number of frames if > 1 (photo) (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Attachment.frames` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Attachment.frames` 0..1 `positiveInt`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Attachment.frames` 0..1 `positiveInt`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Attachment.frames` has a context of Attachment based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Attachment`.
Element `Attachment.frames` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.comment:
Element `Attachment.frames` has a context of Attachment based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Attachment`.
Element `Attachment.frames` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
if the number of frames is not supplied, the value may be unknown. Applications should not assume that there is only one frame unless it is explicitly stated.

element.definition: The number of frames in a photo. This is used with a multi-page fax, or an imaging acquisition context that takes multiple slices in a single image, or an animated gif. If there is more than one frame, this SHALL have a value in order to alert interface software that a multi-frame capable rendering widget is required.
element.comment: if the number of frames is not supplied, the value may be unknown. Applications should not assume that there is only one frame unless it is explicitly stated.
